title: CRYPTOGRAPHY-0001 — public padding.PKCS7(0)/ANSIX923(0) panics with divide/modulo by zero instead of raising ValueError (src/rust/src/padding.rs)
crate: cryptography-rust (the _rust PyO3 module in the cryptography repo)
panic_reason: attempt to divide by zero (unpadder .update, :182/:241) / attempt to calculate the remainder with a divisor of zero (padder .finalize, :103/:147)
expected: PKCS7(0)/ANSIX923(0) should raise ValueError at construction (block_size of 0 is invalid), like every other bad block_size does; it must not build a context that later panics
reliability: deterministic, single call, single-threaded (NOT free-threading related); reproduces on any build (ordinary / and % by zero, not a debug-only overflow check)
target_python: CPython 3.14t free-threaded + ThreadSanitizer build (GIL off); bug is interpreter/build independent
prior_art: UNREPORTED. gh api search/issues on pyca/cryptography (2026-07-21) found no issue for block_size=0, a padding divide-by-zero, or a PanicException from PKCS7/ANSIX923.
notes: _byte_padding_check (src/cryptography/hazmat/primitives/padding.py:32-37) admits block_size=0: `if not (0 <= block_size <= 2040)` (lower bound should be `0 <`) and `0 % 8 == 0`. The Rust contexts store block_size/8 = 0 for block_size<8 and then divide/modulo by it at four sites (PKCS7/ANSIX923 x unpadder-update/padder-finalize). All four reach Python as pyo3_runtime.PanicException. Fix = `0 < block_size` in _byte_padding_check (closes all four at once); optionally also reject block_size==0 in the Rust new() constructors since hazmat.bindings._rust is directly importable. Panic-on-bad-input class caused by a validator off-by-one rather than a missing check. NOT a security/UB issue (caught panic, no memory unsafety), NOT free-threading related.
